message:
fix(doctor): emit pure JSON in robot mode (un-blocks CI E2E)

The E2E `Robot Mode Validation` job has been red on main for ~5 runs.
`ms doctor --robot` was emitting human-readable check output via raw
`println!` (149 sites) regardless of `ctx.output_format`, then the
robot-mode test piped the capture into `jq -e .` and saw a wall of
"ms doctor - Health Checks\n\nChecking lock status... [ok] No lock
held\n..." in front of any structured payload. jq rejected the first
non-JSON line and the CI gate failed.

Pre-existing bug — the regression predates the dependabot-PR sweep
the workflow last ran against; the same job has been red since at
least 2026-04-23.

Fix:

- New file-local `say!` and `say_inline!` macros wrap `println!` /
  `print!` and no-op when `ctx.output_format.is_machine_readable()`
  (json / jsonl / tsv / toon). The macros take `ctx` explicitly so
  they remain greppable and don't smuggle global state through
  thread-locals.
- Replaced every `println!` (149 sites) and every `print!` (~25
  sites) in the doctor command with `say!(ctx, ...)` /
  `say_inline!(ctx, ...)`. `print_recovery_report` gained a `ctx`
  parameter so it can do the same.
- At the end of `doctor::run()`, when machine-readable, we emit a
  single-line JSON envelope on stdout:

      {"status":"ok"|"issues"|"fixed",
       "issues_found":N,"issues_fixed":M,
       "fix_requested":bool,"comprehensive":bool,
       "check":<run-only filter or null>}

  `to_string` (compact) rather than `to_string_pretty` so consumers
  doing line-buffered ndjson capture get exactly one line.

Edge cases:

- Human mode is bit-identical to before (every check still prints
  the same "Checking …" / "[ok] …" / "[!] …" lines and the same
  trailing summary). Verified by running `ms doctor` against a
  freshly-initialised tempdir and diffing against the previous
  binary's output.
- `--check <foo>` filtering, `--fix`, `--comprehensive`, and the
  unknown-check error all flow through unchanged. They surface in
  the JSON envelope as `check`, `fix_requested`, and
  `comprehensive` respectively so robot consumers can correlate.

Verification:
- `ms doctor --robot 2>&1 | jq -e .` exits 0 with the expected shape.
- `ms doctor` (human) output unchanged.
- `cargo build` clean.
- `cargo test --workspace` 3263 passed / 108 failed / 35 ignored —
  exact match to the pre-change baseline (the 108 are pre-existing
  concurrency / environment tests, none doctor-related).
- New integration test `tests/cli.rs::test_doctor_robot_emits_pure_json`
  pins the contract: spawn the binary, capture stdout, assert it
  parses as a single JSON value with `status` ∈ {ok, issues, fixed}
  and numeric `issues_found` / `issues_fixed`. Locks the bug class
  so a future regression like this can't slip through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
